subject: Add functionality for VFC request retries
commit_message:
Add functionality for VFC request retries

This allows the control loop to track operation
attempts in case of VFC failures. This will
allow retries of the operation if required in the
YAML file.

Issue-ID: POLICY-270
Change-Id: Ieeaf6770a8d03fa4eecc976535301d9236d19972
Signed-off-by: Ritu Sood <ritu.sood@intel.com>

looks good to you Ali?

Patch Set 1:

> looks good to you Ali?

Yes, but it seems redundant as there is already code in the VFCManager that sends a restful request in a while loop (while (attemptsLeft-- > 0 where attemptsLeft is hardcoded to 20). Please see line 92 in VFCManager.java

Patch Set 1:

OK, thanks Ali, let's wait till we get feedback from Ritu

Patch Set 1:

> > looks good to you Ali?
 > 
 > Yes, but it seems redundant as there is already code in the
 > VFCManager that sends a restful request in a while loop (while
 > (attemptsLeft-- > 0 where attemptsLeft is hardcoded to 20). Please
 > see line 92 in VFCManager.java

Hi Ali, That is the case when VFC sends HTTP response accepted(202) and the code is waiting for the request to complete and send sucess(200). This is one request only.

 > Hi Ali, That is the case when VFC sends HTTP response accepted(202)
 > and the code is waiting for the request to complete and send
 > sucess(200). This is one request only.

Hi Ritu, 
So what if you get a failure response (i.e 400 bad request)? Then it will retry the request up to 20 times until you get a success, correct?

Patch Set 1:

> > > > looks good to you Ali?
 > > >
 > > > Yes, but it seems redundant as there is already code in the
 > > > VFCManager that sends a restful request in a while loop (while

 > > Please
 > > > see line 92 in VFCManager.java
 > >
 > > Hi Ali, That is the case when VFC sends HTTP response
 > accepted(202)
 > > and the code is waiting for the request to complete and send
 > > sucess(200). This is one request only.
 > 
 > Hi Ritu,
 > So what if you get a failure response (i.e 400 bad request)? Then
 > it will retry the request up to 20 times until you get a success,
 > correct?

Yes that is correct. The attempts here are waiting for one request to be complete and not sending new requests. From VFC perspective only one request was sent.

 > > > and the code is waiting for the request to complete and send

 > >
 > > Hi Ritu,
 > > So what if you get a failure response (i.e 400 bad request)? Then
 > > it will retry the request up to 20 times until you get a success,
 > > correct?
 > 
 > Yes that is correct. The attempts here are waiting for one request
 > to be complete and not sending new requests. From VFC perspective
 > only one request was sent.

Right, and that is the same functionality as the code change you made in ControlLoopOperationManager which is why I think it is redundant. I suggest you keep 1 of these - either get rid of the change you made to ControlLoopOperationManager and keep the while loop in VFCManager and maybe instead of hardcoding to 20 attempts you can grab the 'retry' value from the yaml OR keep the change you made in ControlLoopOperationManager and get rid of the while loop in VFCManager (send the request without while loop). Jorge, what do you think?

Patch Set 1:

> > > > > looks good to you Ali?
 > > > >
 > > > > Yes, but it seems redundant as there is already code in the
 > > > > VFCManager that sends a restful request in a while loop
 > (while
 > > > > (attemptsLeft-- > 0 where attemptsLeft is hardcoded to 20).
 > > > Please
 > > > > see line 92 in VFCManager.java
 > > >
 > > > Hi Ali, That is the case when VFC sends HTTP response
 > > accepted(202)
 > > > and the code is waiting for the request to complete and send
 > > > sucess(200). This is one request only.
 > >
 > > Hi Ritu,
 > > So what if you get a failure response (i.e 400 bad request)? Then
 > > it will retry the request up to 20 times until you get a success,
 > > correct?
 > 
 > Yes that is correct. The attempts here are waiting for one request
 > to be complete and not sending new requests. From VFC perspective
 > only one request was sent.

Hi Ali, In my understanding these two are different things. In VFCManger we are waiting for VFC to finish completing the request. VFC may not be able to complete the request right away. This is for one request. The REST call in the loop here is a GET call based on the jobid received from a POST call made before outside the loop. If the request fails VFC is not going to retry it based on the GET call but keep returning the same result. The YAML file retries is for actually trying again by sending another POST call.

 > success,
 > > > correct?
 > >
 > > Yes that is correct. The attempts here are waiting for one
 > request
 > > to be complete and not sending new requests. From VFC perspective
 > > only one request was sent.
 > 
 > Hi Ali, In my understanding these two are different things. In
 > VFCManger we are waiting for VFC to finish completing the request.
 > VFC may not be able to complete the request right away. This is for
 > one request. The REST call in the loop here is a GET call based on
 > the jobid received from a POST call made before outside the loop.
 > If the request fails VFC is not going to retry it based on the GET
 > call but keep returning the same result. The YAML file retries is
 > for actually trying again by sending another POST call.

Oh, I see.. The while loop in VFCManager is only retrying the GET call whereas the change made in ControlLoopOperationManager will retry the operation by sending another POST call. Got it, looks good to me then!
Change has been successfully merged by Jorge Hernandez
